title: Moist Hangover-Curing Ethiopian Beef Brisket Dabo
description: A rich and savory Ethiopian beef brisket dish featuring a hangover-curing dashi broth, anti-inflammatory spices, and a gooey cheese pull. Perfect for a post-party brunch.

ingredients.name: beef brisket
ingredients.notes: first cut, with a good amount of fat

ingredients.name: Ethiopian spice blend
ingredients.notes: includes cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and cardamom

ingredients.name: shredded cheese
ingredients.notes: Monterey Jack or other melty cheese

instructions: Season the beef brisket with the Ethiopian spice blend, cayenne pepper, and black pepper.
Heat the ghee or oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the beef brisket until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the brisket to a plate and set aside. Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in the dashi broth and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Return the beef brisket to the pot and cover with a lid. Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and braise the brisket for 2 hours, or until tender.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly.
Shred the beef brisket with two forks and place it back in the pot.
Stir in the shredded cheese until melted and gooey.
Pile the beef and cheese mixture onto flatbread or injera, and serve immediately.
